text.title: xUnit Testing Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure to produce a test class with the right scope, fixture, mocks, and assertions.

step.name: pick-scope
step.action: Apply decision tree: pure logic → unit; controller + DI graph → integration via WebApplicationFactory; repository → slice with SQLite-in-memory or Testcontainers.

step.name: fixture-and-host
step.action: Unit → no fixture. Integration → `IClassFixture<WebApplicationFactory<Program>>`; override `ConfigureWebHost` to swap DbContext for SQLite-in-memory; add `public partial class Program {}` to API project.
step.decision-gate: If `Program` is internal in API project, FAIL until the partial class shim is added.

step.name: write-test-methods
step.action: Name each test `Method_Input_Expected`. Async tests return `Task`. Parametric cases use `[Theory] + [InlineData(...)]`. AAA layout with blank-line separators.

step.name: mocks
step.input: Collaborators that should not be exercised
step.action: Mock via Moq with precise `It.Is<T>(...)` matchers. Verify expected calls with `mock.Verify(...)`. Avoid `It.IsAny<T>()` unless argument is genuinely irrelevant.

step.name: assertions
step.action: Use FluentAssertions on objects/collections/responses: `result.Should().BeOfType<OkObjectResult>().Which.Value.Should().BeEquivalentTo(expected)`. Primitives may use `Should().Be(...)`.
step.decision-gate: If FluentAssertions are absent on object asserts, FAIL the review.
